reason: A 66/100 score is broadly consistent with fan and critic consensus. Multiple sources confirm significant losses in motivations/backstory (Gaunt family history, love potion origin, Tom Riddle's charisma all heavily cut), personality (film Voldemort is more flamboyant and dramatic vs the books' cold, calculating precision), and arc (death scene changed, no Great Hall moment, no 'Tom' taunting reaction). The low motivation score (11/25) is well-supported - this is the most-cited criticism. The narrative role score (14/25) is slightly harder to justify since Voldemort remains the central antagonist in both mediums; he loses depth but not his structural role. The personality score (16/25) seems reasonable given Ralph Fiennes' performance preserved menace even if the subtlety was lost. Overall the total and dimension breakdown align with what critics say, though narrative_role could arguably be a point or two higher.
